summary:
Funded Trading Plus (FTP) scores 77/100 in our 2026 review. The firm currently offers three core programmes — Instant Funding, 1-Step Express, and 2-Step Classic — replacing their earlier lineup of six legacy models. All trading takes place in a simulated environment, with payouts based on simulated profits.

The standard profit split is 80%, upgradable to 90% or even 100% depending on account type or add-ons. Payout schedules vary by programme: Instant Funding and 1-Step Express allow withdrawals from day 1 then every 7 days, while 2-Step Classic uses a 10-day payout cycle. Drawdown rules also differ — Instant Funding uses 6% daily/max (relative), while 1-Step Express uses 4% daily and 6% max (relative), and 2-Step Classic uses 4% daily and 8% max (static). Scaling is available up to $2.5 million. FTP remains a strong option for traders who want variety and flexible evaluation structures, though limited spread transparency holds the score back.
pros: Profit split from 80% up to 100% with upgrades
Instant funding option available — skip the evaluation entirely
Three programme types for different trading styles
Instant Funding and 1-Step Express allow withdrawals from day 1
Four trading platforms: DXTrade, Match-Trader, cTrader, MT5
cons: Minimal transparency on spreads and trading costs
Higher fees for instant funding accounts
DXTrade has reported lag and connectivity issues
Copy trading across accounts is prohibited
bestFor: Experienced traders who want flexible evaluation structures with instant funding, one-step, and two-step options, scaling potential up to $2.5 million, and profit splits upgradable to 100%
notIdealFor: Traders who need clear spread data upfront, beginners seeking comprehensive educational resources, those who rely on copy trading across accounts, or traders who want uniform rules across all programmes

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On Funded Trading Plus
verdictBlocks.body:
Funded Trading Plus provides a solid option for traders seeking access to simulated capital through a streamlined evaluation process. All trading takes place in a simulated environment, with payouts based on simulated profits.

With three core programmes — Instant Funding, 1-Step Express, and 2-Step Classic — FTP offers flexibility without the complexity of its earlier six-model lineup. The standard 80% profit split can be upgraded to 90% or 100%, and the scaling plan allows accounts to grow up to $2.5 million.

Drawdown and payout rules vary by programme, so traders should review the specific terms for their chosen model. Copy trading across accounts is prohibited, and a 30-day inactivity rule can breach accounts. The 2-Step Classic includes a consistency rule. The lack of transparency on trading costs and reported issues with the DXTrade platform remain the main drawbacks.

Overall, Funded Trading Plus is a strong contender for experienced traders who value programme flexibility and scaling potential, and who can navigate programme-specific rules.

faq.a: Funded Trading Plus is generally considered a legitimate prop firm with a large and active trading community and a long operating history compared to many newer firms. It offers clearly defined challenge structures, documented rules, and a transparent account dashboard. All trading takes place in a simulated environment — traders do not manage real capital. FTP is not a broker-backed prop firm and some traders raise concerns around spread transparency and platform consistency.

faq.q: Does Funded Trading Plus offer a scaling plan?
faq.a: Yes. Funded Trading Plus offers a performance-based scaling plan, allowing accounts to grow up to $2.5 million based on profit milestones. Scaling is optional and based on hitting predefined profit targets on your funded account. There are no time-based requirements, but higher account sizes and payout conditions depend on the specific programme you choose and continued rule compliance.

Yes. Funded Trading Plus does pay out profits based on simulated trading performance, provided you follow the trading rules. Payout schedules vary by programme: Instant Funding and 1-Step Express allow withdrawals from day 1 then every 7 days; 2-Step Classic uses a 10-day payout cycle. Withdrawals are available via bank transfer or cryptocurrency, with a minimum payout amount of $50.

faq.q: What are the profit split and fees for Funded Trading Plus?
faq.a: The standard profit split is 80%, but depending on the programme and any optional upgrades at checkout, you can increase it to 90% or even 100%. Challenge fees vary by programme and account size. Instant Funding accounts include sizes from $5,000 to $100,000, while 1-Step Express and 2-Step Classic start at $10,000 and go up to $200,000.

faq.q: Which trading platforms does Funded Trading Plus offer?
faq.a: Funded Trading Plus currently offers four platforms: DXTrade, Match-Trader, MetaTrader 5 (MT5), and cTrader. MT5 is well suited if you use Expert Advisors or custom indicators. Match-Trader offers TradingView integration and works well for discretionary trading. cTrader provides advanced tools but carries a $25 platform fee. DXTrade is available, though it is generally less popular due to performance limitations.

faq.q: Is copy trading allowed at Funded Trading Plus?
faq.a: No. Copy trading across accounts is prohibited at Funded Trading Plus, including copying between FTP accounts or mirroring trades across multiple accounts.
